metrics.text: Revenue $7.5M, 28 employees. 500–1000 orders/day at peak. PBR $120K. Nov 2026 go-live target drives urgency — 5-6 month build means July contract start is the drop-dead point. Everest ERP EOL is the existential forcing function.

economic_buyer.text: Tony Meirovitz, VP of E-commerce — confirmed primary DM (Tim Katz: 'he trusts us so much, not one iota of debate'). Tony has BigCommerce background from prior role but DYODE and Intech's own Shopify setup have already reaffirmed Shopify direction. Tony is joining the May 20 discovery call directly.

decision_criteria.text: Modern website redesign + ease of use for internal team. Unique branded B2B customer portal per company (Shopify B2B company model confirmed as fit). Flat file ERP integration interim until NetSuite is ready (Everest integration dropped). Must replicate custom modules: cable configurator, RA/repairs, Cash for Course buyback, sell-parts. SI partner required for build.

decision_process.text: SE Jordan Murphy assigned — already prepping integration approach. Technical discovery May 20 4pm with Tony Meirovitz, Jordan Murphy, DYODE (Tim Katz, Elana K.). Evaluation hub shared with DYODE and being forwarded to Tony pre-call. Tim Katz requesting contract reviews start in parallel within 2 weeks. Formal procurement path not yet mapped.

identified_pain.text: Everest ERP reaching end-of-life — no long-term support path. Current custom .NET platform requires full rebuild. Business pain is existential: must migrate entire ecommerce + ERP stack by Dec 2026. Highly specialized custom modules add complexity. Website is outdated — internal team cannot make basic updates without dev support.

champion.text: DYODE team (Tim Katz, co-founder; Elana K., Director Ops & Product) are internal advocates driving the introduction and actively pushing Shopify direction. Tony Meirovitz (VP E-comm) is DM — trusts DYODE's recommendation fully. Intech (sister company) already setting up Shopify Basic store, which strengthens Shopify preference going into Tony's first call.

competition.text: BigCommerce — Tony's previous company used BigCommerce. Tim Katz and Elana K. are firmly Shopify ('not one iota of debate'). Evaluation hub includes Shopify vs BigCommerce comparison at DYODE's request to help Tony confirm direction.

national-gym-supply.strategy: Strong DYODE-sourced deal with confirmed DM (Tony) joining May 20. Priority: nail the discovery call — surface Tony's specific requirements, validate custom module scope with Jordan Murphy, and confirm contract start timeline. Tim Katz wants parallel contract pathing within 2 weeks — push for a Jul 15 close. DYODE relationship is the key asset to protect.

risks.text: Custom modules (cable configurator, RA/repairs, Cash for Course) — scope complexity unknown
risks.mit: Jordan Murphy to assess module complexity on May 20 discovery call. Identify SI partner options early.

risks.text: Contract review parallel path — Tim Katz requested within 2 weeks; Shopify legal/commercial not yet engaged
risks.mit: Kick off contract review process after May 20 call. Loop in Shopify commercial team ASAP.

risks.text: Jul 15 close date is aggressive for Envision stage — design + scoping must complete before contract
risks.mit: Align on realistic timeline at May 20 — confirm July start is achievable given 5-6 month build.

risks.text: Flat file ERP interim approach — technical unknowns around data structure and Shopify integration points
risks.mit: Jordan Murphy to define flat file integration spec at discovery call. Document before contract signed.

done: Requirements doc received via DYODE — full custom module requirements documented
Key stakeholders identified: Tony Meirovitz (VP E-comm / DM), Tim Katz (DYODE), Elana K. (DYODE)
DYODE Reconnect May 15 — Tony DM confirmed, evaluation hub shared and accepted, flat file ERP approach aligned, SE Jordan Murphy engaged
Evaluation hub (national-gym-supply-shopify.quick.shopify.io) built and shared with DYODE for Tony
Intech (sister co.) on Shopify Basic — strengthens Shopify preference entering discovery
left: Lead discovery call May 20 4pm — surface Tony's requirements, validate custom module scope with Jordan Murphy
Kick off contract review parallel path (Tim Katz requested within 2 weeks of May 15)
Update evaluation hub with flat file ERP approach and accelerated July timeline
Confirm Jul 15 close date with Tony on May 20 call
Scope custom module complexity and identify SI partner
national-gym-supply.notes: Introduced via DYODE partner (Tim Katz). Everest ERP EOL is the forcing function. Company does 500–1000 peak orders/day, $7.5M revenue, 28 employees, Louisville CO area. Highly specialized custom modules: cable configurator, RA/repairs, Cash for Course buyback, sell-parts. Evaluation hub shared pre-call. Tony joining May 20 discovery call.

manager_q.compelling_event: Everest ERP EOL — must migrate by Dec 2026. November go-live target.
manager_q.urgency: July build start required for Nov launch — contract must close by mid-July
manager_q.risk_to_close: Custom module scope unknown; contract review not yet started

metrics.text: DTC online revenue ~$500K (excl. ~$1.5M acquisition brand just added). B2B online only ~$50K last year — vast majority of B2B done offline through reps/fax/email. Cost comparison: NetSuite SuiteCommerce = 3x current Magento spend. Total wholesale revenue unknown (Alli does not own that P&L).

economic_buyer.text: Not yet identified above Alli Boggs. Alli (Director of E-commerce) is primary contact and evaluator but flagged cost as a constraint she cannot unilaterally approve — final sign-off is above her. Buying committee not yet mapped.

decision_criteria.text: Must be cheaper than NetSuite SuiteCommerce (~3x Magento). Requires: NetSuite ERP feed for custom pricing + case pack configs, no-code site updates (Sidekick/AI), sales rep portal access to customer orders and tracking. Plus vs Advanced still an open question given DTC revenue size ($500K).

decision_process.text: Mercedes to send Plus vs Advanced comparison materials by Mon May 11. Alli to review. Technical follow-up scheduled after Allen DTC site #2 launches (~May 12–13). Broader buying committee and paper process not yet mapped.

identified_pain.text: Adobe Commerce (Magento) requires dev/IT consultant for simple site changes (banners, video). Two separate Magento instances (DTC + B2B) = operational overhead. B2B portal adoption near zero — reps still use fax/email/CS manually. Evaluating NetSuite SuiteCommerce as B2B replacement but it is 3x cost and Alli is not excited about it.

champion.text: Alli Boggs (Director of E-commerce). Engaged and enthusiastic about Shopify, driving the evaluation. Low financial authority — final say likely above her. Julie Marcott (AE buddy) co-owns relationship and joined May 7 call.

allen-company.strategy: DTC migration to Shopify Advanced is already happening across 4–5 brands. The strategic prize is native B2B on Plus — winning this before Alli gets locked into NetSuite SuiteCommerce. Competitive window: Alli explicitly said she is not excited about NetSuite for B2B. Priority: Plus vs Advanced comparison by Mon May 11, then technical call with SE to close the loop on case packs + ERP feed objections.

risks.text: Economic buyer not identified — Alli may not have budget authority for Plus
risks.mit: Identify EB on next technical call. Frame Plus ROI around B2B growth and dealer adoption to get exec attention.

risks.text: DTC revenue ($500K) may not justify Plus pricing — Advanced may be ceiling
risks.mit: Model Plus pricing relative to B2B growth potential. If Plus requires >$30K/year, deal may stay on Advanced.

risks.text: B2B timeline undefined — DTC migration could push B2B decision to Q4+
risks.mit: Send materials by Mon May 11, schedule technical call proactively. Don't let B2B become a 2027 conversation.

risks.text: NetSuite SuiteCommerce still on table as B2B option
risks.mit: Price delta (3x) is our best defense. Keep SuiteCommerce comparison explicit in materials.

done: Discovery call May 7 — Adobe Commerce pain documented, B2B requirements surfaced
Confirmed Shopify Payments eligibility (supportability doc complete, cleared May 5)
Confirmed Shopify supports: custom pricing per account, case pack configs, NetSuite ERP feed, sales rep portal
Identified competition: NetSuite SuiteCommerce (3x cost, low enthusiasm from Alli)
left: Send Plus vs Advanced comparison materials by Mon May 11 (action item from call)
Schedule technical follow-up after Allen DTC site #2 launches (~May 12–13)
Identify economic buyer above Alli Boggs
Map full buying committee before technical call
Get Alli to send existing Shopify site URLs
allen-company.notes: Director of E-commerce Alli Boggs (allison.boggs@allencompany.net) is primary contact. DTC already migrating to Shopify Advanced across 4–5 brands. B2B is the strategic play — currently $50K online but large offline dealer network (gun shops, ranges) not digitized. ERP: NetSuite. DTC off Magento by mid-to-late June. Competing with NetSuite SuiteCommerce which Alli finds uninspiring and 3x cost. Shopify Payments cleared May 5.

metrics.text: Revenue ~$35M, ~200 employees, London HQ. 90% of revenue is wholesale — almost entirely undigitized. ROI hypothesis: digitizing wholesale ordering could significantly reduce manual CS overhead and improve buyer experience for international accounts. No metrics quantified yet.

economic_buyer.text: Not yet identified. Nicole Freydell is primary contact (role/seniority unclear). No executive sponsor or budget owner mapped.

decision_criteria.text: Not yet fully surfaced. Known needs: B2B portal for specialty coffee wholesale buyers, integration requirements TBD (IT Director joining next call), multi-currency likely required (London HQ, international accounts).

decision_process.text: Transferred from Grant Dugas (SDR, who declined intro meeting). Intro call Apr 29 with Nicole. Technical deep dive pending — IT Director joining next call. Formal eval criteria, competing platforms, and wider stakeholders beyond Nicole all unknown.

identified_pain.text: 90% of business is wholesale and almost none of it is digitized — customers ordering manually. WooCommerce DTC site not meeting B2B needs. Nicole confirmed desire for a B2B wholesale portal to replace manual processes.

champion.text: Nicole Freydell is engaged but title, seniority, and internal influence are unknown. No confirmed champion with budget authority.

caravela-coffee.strategy: THIN — 2/16 MEDDPICC. This deal needs discovery depth before it can advance. Priority on next call: surface economic buyer and IT Director's authority, quantify the wholesale order volume pain (how many orders/day, how much manual work), and identify competing platforms being evaluated. The 90% wholesale angle is a perfect B2B story — but it needs a champion with authority.

risks.text: THIN QUALIFICATION: EB, competition, and decision criteria all unknown
risks.mit: Next call must surface EB and IT Director's role. Treat as a discovery call, not a demo.

risks.text: Transferred deal — Nicole's context and motivation are unclear
risks.mit: Understand why Grant declined the intro. Ensure Nicole has real authority to drive a decision.

risks.text: International HQ (London) may introduce VAT/compliance and multi-currency complexity
risks.mit: Confirm region and currency requirements before proposing solution.

risks.text: No stated internal deadline or project trigger
risks.mit: Surface urgency on next call — what's forcing action now?

done: Intro call Apr 29 — confirmed B2B wholesale need, platform is WooCommerce (DTC)
Technical deep dive scheduled with IT Director joining
left: Surface economic buyer on next call
Understand IT Director's role and technical requirements
Quantify wholesale order volume and manual processing overhead
Identify competing platforms being evaluated
Map buying committee and decision criteria
caravela-coffee.notes: Specialty coffee B2B wholesale, 90% wholesale, $35M rev, ~200 employees, London HQ. Contact: Nicole Freydell (nicole.freydell@caravela.coffee). Transferred from Grant Dugas (SDR). Current platform: WooCommerce (DTC only). IT Director joining next call — critical discovery session. International business — confirm multi-currency, VAT, and ERP context.

metrics.text: PBR $165K. Business revenue, order volume, and SKU count not yet captured in discovery. ROI case not yet built — integration replacement (Salesforce + Sage) is the stated driver.

economic_buyer.text: Not yet identified. Kyle Boyd (kyle@artificialturfsupply.com) is primary contact but title and budget authority unknown. No executive sponsor mapped.

decision_criteria.text: Q4 go-live target (Oct 2026). Build starts July. Must integrate with Salesforce (CRM) and Sage (ERP/accounting) to replace legacy systems. Specific integration data flows not yet documented.

decision_process.text: Technical discovery completed May 5 with Kyle Boyd and Jordan Murphy (SE). Next meeting first week of July after build scope is confirmed. Formal eval process, competitive shortlist, and other stakeholders not yet mapped.

identified_pain.text: Legacy Salesforce + Sage integrations require replacement — current setup creating operational friction. Q4 switchover is a stated business target with build starting July, indicating real urgency to decide quickly.

champion.text: Kyle Boyd is engaged and actively moving the deal forward with Jordan Murphy (SE). Authority level not confirmed. Jordan Murphy is engaged as SE champion on the Shopify side.

artificial-turf-supply.strategy: THIN — 4/16 MEDDPICC. SE Jordan Murphy is the key asset here — leverage the July meeting to surface economic buyer, build the ROI case, and nail down integration scope for Salesforce + Sage before the July build start. The July gap (now to July) is 6+ weeks dark — stay in touch with Kyle to maintain momentum.

risks.text: THIN QUALIFICATION: EB not identified, metrics not captured
risks.mit: July meeting must surface EB and business case. Prep Jordan with EB discovery questions.

risks.text: 6-week gap May → July — deal can go cold
risks.mit: Monthly check-in with Kyle. Send value-add content (integration docs, case studies) to stay top of mind.

risks.text: Salesforce + Sage integration complexity could extend timeline past Q4
risks.mit: SE Jordan Murphy to map integration scope in July session. Identify certified SI partner early.

risks.text: No competitive intel — unknown what alternatives are being evaluated
risks.mit: Ask Kyle directly on next check-in: who else are you talking to?

done: Technical discovery call May 5 with Kyle Boyd + Jordan Murphy (SE)
Integration requirements identified: Salesforce + Sage (replace legacy)
Q4 switchover target and July build start confirmed
left: Surface economic buyer at or before July meeting
Quantify business metrics and build ROI case
Map Salesforce + Sage integration scope with Jordan Murphy (SE)
Identify competing platforms
Stay in touch with Kyle during May–July dark period
artificial-turf-supply.notes: Contact: Kyle Boyd (kyle@artificialturfsupply.com). SE: Jordan Murphy (jordan.murphy@shopify.com). Technical discovery May 5. Build starts July, Q4 go-live target. Integrations: Salesforce (CRM) + Sage (ERP/accounting) replacing legacy. Next meeting 1st week of July.
